title: [Bug]: HTTP sink must be accepted by routing target sink-type validation
summary: The first-party HTTP sink was added to fan-out optional ACK defaults, but the routing policy configuration still rejected `http` as a valid target sink type.
observed: The full repository validation failed in the routing policy test that iterates over every optional ACK default sink type. The `http` entry reached the route policy model and was rejected before validation could apply the HTTP defaults.
expected: Routing target sink-type validation should accept every sink type that has optional ACK defaults, including `http`, so route policies can be validated consistently.
reproduction: Run the routing policy unit test that checks optional ACK defaults for each sink type. The HTTP parameter fails during configuration validation.
failing_test: `python -m pytest tests/unit/test_routing_policy.py::test_route_policy_applies_sink_type_optional_ack_gate_defaults -q` fails for the `http` parameter before the fix.
impact: Operators could not use the new HTTP sink as an optional fan-out route target even though the core fan-out defaults declared HTTP support. Required HTTP routing through direct named sink selection was unaffected.
delivery_semantics: The defect prevented valid route configuration from loading. It did not cause early ACKs, sink writes, DLQ changes, or message loss.
security: The fix expands a strict allow list by one first-party sink type only. It does not add dynamic imports, message-controlled destinations, unbounded fan-out, or secret exposure.
acceptance: Routing target sink-type validation accepts `http` alongside the other first-party sink types.
The optional ACK default coverage test passes for the HTTP sink type.
Invalid sink types remain rejected by route policy validation.
Focused routing policy tests pass after the fix.
The full repository validation passes after the fix.
tests: Run python -m pytest tests/unit/test_routing_policy.py::test_route_policy_applies_sink_type_optional_ack_gate_defaults tests/unit/test_routing_policy.py::test_route_policy_rejects_unknown_target_sink_type -q, then rerun scripts/check.sh.
documentation: No user-facing documentation change is required beyond the HTTP sink documentation already added for the parent feature; this bug is a configuration allow-list consistency fix.
closeout: Close after the v0.4.2 release containing the routing validation fix is published. Include sanitized focused and full validation evidence.
